requirements.task_name: Create a Python quickstart evaluation with accuracy and F1 on 200 samples
requirements.task_description: Using the developer docs, locate the Python quickstart for text classification, configure an evaluation that uses both accuracy and F1 metrics, sets max_samples to 200, uses model id "gpt-4.1-mini", and copy the final code snippet.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyCodeSnippet is called for a code snippet whose language is 'python' and whose docPage matches the Quickstart Text Classification page (i.e., docPage.category === 'getting_started' and docPage.name lowercased includes 'quickstart', 'text', and 'classification').
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { codeSnippetId: snippet.id, docPageId: snippet.docPageId, language: snippet.language, metrics: evalConfig ? evalConfig.metrics : null, maxSamples: evalConfig ? evalConfig.maxSamples : null, modelId: evalConfig ? evalConfig.modelId : null, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where evalConfig is the evaluation_config_snippets entry for this codeSnippetId, if any.
required_variables.reason: This records that the user copied the Python quickstart text classification snippet and captures the configuration (metrics, maxSamples, modelId) at the moment of copying, allowing the evaluator to verify that it exactly matches ['accuracy','f1'], 200, and 'gpt-4.1-mini'.

requirements.task_name: Build a curl command for the batch evaluation REST endpoint with limit 50 and cursor "cursor_abc"
requirements.task_description: Use the API reference in the docs to find the REST endpoint for creating a batch evaluation run, then construct a curl example that sets project_id to "proj_123" and includes query parameters limit=50 and cursor="cursor_abc", and copy the final curl command.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyApiExample is called for an API example where example.language === 'curl' and the resolved apiEndpoint.name (via _resolveApiExample/_resolveApiEndpoint) indicates it is the batch evaluation creation endpoint (e.g., name lowercased includes 'batch' and 'evaluation').
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { apiExampleId: example.id, apiEndpointId: example.apiEndpointId, code: example.code, extractedProjectId: parsedProjectIdOrNull, extractedLimit: parsedLimitOrNull, extractedCursor: parsedCursorOrNull, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where parsedProjectId/parsedLimit/parsedCursor are extracted from example.code (e.g., via regex on the URL and query string).
required_variables.reason: This captures which curl example was copied, plus the effective project_id, limit, and cursor as present in the command at copy time, enabling verification that the user produced and copied a curl command with project_id='proj_123', limit=50, and cursor='cursor_abc'.

requirements.task_name: Select the cost metric for token-based billing and copy its JSON configuration example
requirements.task_description: From the metrics documentation, choose the metric whose description mentions token-based billing (the cost metric), update its JSON configuration example so price_per_1k_tokens is 0.002 with currency "USD" and unit "tokens", and copy the JSON.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyCodeSnippet is called for a code snippet representing a cost metric JSON configuration example, i.e., snippet.relatedEntityId === 'cost'.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { codeSnippetId: snippet.id, metricId: snippet.relatedEntityId, price_per_1k_tokens: parsed.price_per_1k_tokens || null, currency: parsed.currency || null, unit: parsed.unit || null, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where parsed is the result of JSON.parse(snippet.code) if parsing succeeds, otherwise fields are inferred via simple regex replacements as in configureCostMetricJsonConfig.
required_variables.reason: This records that the user copied the cost metric JSON snippet and captures the key configuration fields (price_per_1k_tokens, currency, unit) at copy time so the evaluator can verify they were set to 0.002, 'USD', and 'tokens'.

requirements.task_name: Configure a comparison evaluation with two candidate models and a baseline using the config builder
requirements.task_description: Using the interactive config builder in the docs, create a text generation evaluation that compares models "candidate_a" and "candidate_b" against baseline "baseline_v1", sets temperatures to 0.2, 0.7, and 0.0 respectively, limits max_samples to 50, enables only accuracy and latency metrics, and generates the config.

requirements.task_name: Find the JavaScript SDK batch evaluation example and set concurrency to 10
requirements.task_description: Using the docs search, locate the JavaScript SDK page that shows the runBatchEvaluation helper, modify the example so the concurrency option is set to 10, and copy the updated JavaScript snippet.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyCodeSnippet is called for the JavaScript SDK runBatchEvaluation example snippet, i.e., snippet.id === 'code_sdk_js_run_batch_evaluation' or snippet.relatedEntityId === 'sdk_js_runBatchEvaluation'.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { codeSnippetId: snippet.id, concurrency: parsedConcurrencyOrNull, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where parsedConcurrencyOrNull is obtained by regex (e.g., matching /concurrency\s*:\s*(\d+)/ on snippet.code).
required_variables.reason: This records that the user copied the JavaScript runBatchEvaluation example and captures the concurrency value at that moment, allowing verification that concurrency was set to 10.

requirements.task_name: Locate a BLEU-based evaluation example with at least 1000 samples and copy its endpoint path
requirements.task_description: In the examples gallery, filter to show only evaluations using the BLEU metric and datasets with a minimum of 1000 samples, open the third matching example, and copy the REST API endpoint path shown on that page.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyEvaluationExampleEndpointPath(evaluationExampleId) returns success === true (i.e., an endpointPath exists).
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { evaluationExampleId, endpointPath: example.endpointPath, filterStateAtCopy: { selectedMetricId: state.selectedMetricId, minDatasetSize: state.minDatasetSize }, filteredIndex: idxInFilteredList, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where state is obtained from _getExamplesFilterStateRecord(), and idxInFilteredList is the index of this example in the current getFilteredEvaluationExamples() result (or -1 if not found).
required_variables.reason: This records which example's endpoint was copied, under which metric and dataset-size filters, and where it appeared in the filtered list. The evaluator can then ensure that the filters were BLEU + min 1000 and that the copied example was the third matching example.

requirements.task_name: Switch docs to API version v2 and copy the dataset creation endpoint curl example
requirements.task_description: Change the documentation version from v1 to v2, open the API reference for the dataset creation endpoint, and copy the curl example that calls the v2 dataset creation path.

required_variables.set_condition: When copyApiExample is called for the synthetic v2 Create dataset curl example, i.e., apiExampleId === 'api_example_datasets_create_v2_curl_request' or example.apiEndpointId === 'datasets_create_v2'.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { apiExampleId: example.id, apiEndpointId: example.apiEndpointId, apiVersion: resolvedEndpoint.apiVersion, pathTemplate: resolvedEndpoint.pathTemplate, code: example.code, copiedAt: new Date().toISOString() }, where resolvedEndpoint is obtained via _resolveApiEndpoint for example.apiEndpointId.
required_variables.reason: This captures that the user copied the v2 dataset creation curl example, and ties it to the v2 endpoint definition (including pathTemplate and version), allowing the evaluator to confirm that the copied command targets the v2 dataset creation path.

requirements.task_name: Run a sample evaluation in the playground with weighted accuracy (0.7) and latency (0.3)
requirements.task_description: Using the interactive playground in the docs, configure a text classification evaluation with accuracy weight 0.7 and latency weight 0.3, a sample size of 10, the evaluation name "weighted_test_01", and run the evaluation.
